---
title: "figure3 – Opportunity Map"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = normalizePath(".."))
```

```{r opportunity_map}

dir.create("figures/main", showWarnings = FALSE)

library(tidyverse)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(sf)

# ── 1. Load classification datasets ──────────────────────────────────────────

"%ni%" = Negate("%in%")

papers <- read.csv2("output/paperClassifYear.csv",
                    header = T, sep = ",", dec = ".")

surveys <- read.csv2("output/surveyClassifYear.csv",
                     header = T, sep = ",", dec = ".")

dataSummary <- read.csv("output/datasetPlots.csv")

papers <- papers %>%
  filter(!is.na(iso3))

surveys <- surveys %>%
  filter(!is.na(iso3))

dataSummary <- dataSummary %>%
  filter(!is.na(iso3))

dataSummary$categoryPollution <- ifelse(
  !is.na(dataSummary$categoryPollution),
  paste0(toupper(substr(dataSummary$categoryPollution, 1, 1)),
         substr(dataSummary$categoryPollution, 2, nchar(dataSummary$categoryPollution))),
  NA
)

dataSummary$categoryPollution <- factor(
  dataSummary$categoryPollution,
  levels = rev(c(
    "Very high",
    "High",
    "Medium",
    "Low",
    "Very low"
  )),
  ordered = TRUE
)

papers  <- merge(papers,  dataSummary, by = "iso3", all.x = T, all.y = F)
surveys <- merge(surveys, dataSummary, by = "iso3", all.x = T, all.y = F)

## surveys: drop items for which Inclusion == 0
surveys <- surveys[which(surveys$Inclusion == 1),]

topics <- c("Perceptions", "Priority", "Policy", "Health", "Behavior")

# ── 2. For each country × topic: does ≥1 paper cover it? ─────────────────────
# papers is already long-format (one row per paper × country × category)

lit_covered <- papers %>%
  filter(category %in% topics) %>%
  distinct(iso3, category) %>%
  mutate(lit_covered = 1L) %>%
  rename(topic = category)

# ── 3. For each country × topic: does ≥1 survey item cover it? ───────────────
# surveys is already long-format (one row per item × country × category)

surv_covered <- surveys %>%
  filter(category %in% topics) %>%
  distinct(iso3, category) %>%
  mutate(surv_covered = 1L) %>%
  rename(topic = category)

# ── 4. Assign coverage status (mutually exclusive, priority: lit > survey) ────

opportunity <- full_join(lit_covered, surv_covered, by = c("iso3", "topic")) %>%
  replace_na(list(lit_covered = 0, surv_covered = 0)) %>%
  mutate(status = case_when(
    lit_covered  == 1 ~ "Covered in literature",
    surv_covered == 1 ~ "Survey opportunity",
    TRUE              ~ "No data"
  ))

# ── 5. Build world geometry (sf, no terra needed for faceting) ────────────────

world_sf <- ne_countries(scale = "large", returnclass = "sf")

# France and Norway iso_a3 fix (same as figure1.Rmd)
world_sf$iso_a3 <- ifelse(world_sf$iso_a3_eh == "FRA", "FRA",
                           ifelse(world_sf$iso_a3_eh == "NOR", "NOR",
                                  world_sf$iso_a3))

world_sf <- world_sf %>%
  filter(name != "Antarctica") %>%
  select(iso_a3, geometry)

# ── 6. Expand world to one row per topic, then join opportunity data ──────────

world_expanded <- bind_rows(
  lapply(topics, function(t) world_sf %>% mutate(topic = t))
)

world_opp <- world_expanded %>%
  left_join(
    opportunity %>% select(iso3, topic, status),
    by = c("iso_a3" = "iso3", "topic")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    status = replace_na(status, "No data"),
    status = factor(status,
                    levels = c("Covered in literature",
                               "Survey opportunity",
                               "No data")),
    topic  = factor(topic, levels = topics)
  )

# ── 7. Plot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

status_colors <- c(
  "Covered in literature" = "#8B475D",
  "Survey opportunity"    = "#F7B32B",
  "No data"               = "#EEEEEE"
)

map_opp <- ggplot(world_opp) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = status), color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = status_colors, name = "") +
  facet_wrap(~ topic, nrow = 2) +
  coord_sf(expand = FALSE) +
  theme_void(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "bottom",
    legend.key.size   = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.text       = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.text        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11, margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.background   = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  )

map_opp

# ── 8. Save ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ggsave(
  plot     = map_opp,
  filename = "figures/main/Figure3_opportunity_map.png",
  dpi      = 600,
  width    = 24,
  height   = 14,
  units    = "cm"
)

pdf("figures/main/Figure3_opportunity_map.pdf", width = 14, height = 8)
print(map_opp)
dev.off()

```
